title: Get $341 off the limited X edition of the Vaonis Vespera II at Walmart
summary: Walmart is offering a $341 discount on the limited X edition of the Vaonis Vespera II telescope. The device includes a transparent shell and internal temperature sensors for improved deep sky observation.

title: With the Vespera III and Vespera Pro 2, telescope-maker Vaonis unveils its sharpest optics yet
summary: Vaonis has introduced the Vespera III and Vespera Pro 2 telescopes, featuring sharper optics and upgraded features for improved stargazing. The new models are now available for purchase.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 210 — ESCAPADES at Mars
summary: Episode 210 of the This Week In Space podcast discusses the Mars ESCAPADE mission with Dr. Robert Lillis. The podcast covers updates and insights about the ESCAPADE mission to Mars.

title: NASA's Psyche spacecraft is flying to a metal asteroid. So, why did it just visit Mars?
summary: NASA's Psyche spacecraft performed a flyby of Mars to utilize a gravity assist, which helps it gain speed and conserve fuel for its journey to a metal asteroid. This maneuver is a common technique in space exploration to optimize spacecraft trajectories.

title: Astronomers find interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS hiding in images taken before its official discovery
summary: Astronomers discovered that the interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS was previously spotted in images taken by a giant survey telescope over a week before its official discovery. The comet was almost named 3I/Rubin after these pre-discovery observations.

title: The devil may wear Prada on Earth, but NASA Artemis astronauts will on the moon. Here's why.
summary: NASA's Artemis astronauts will wear Prada-designed spacesuits on the moon, highlighting a collaboration between fashion and space exploration. The connection between the fashion industry in 'The Devil Wears Prada 2' and NASA is a playful reference to this unexpected partnership.

title: The Canon EOS R5 Mark II is our choice as the best-resolution Canon camera – it's perfect for astrophotography and is $501 off in this Walmart deal
summary: The Canon EOS R5 Mark II is praised as the best-resolution Canon camera for astrophotography and is currently discounted by $501 at Walmart. Expert James Abbott gave it a flawless review, highlighting its impressive performance and all-round capabilities.

title: Mars orbiter captures striking images of 'chaos and craters' carved by ancient floods
summary: ESA's Mars Express orbiter captured dramatic images of a rugged Martian landscape shaped by ancient catastrophic floods billions of years ago. The images reveal striking details of 'chaos and craters' carved by these ancient events.

title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch on 34th SpaceX Resupply Mission to Station
summary: NASA's 34th SpaceX resupply mission launched to the International Space Station with new scientific experiments aboard a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral. The spacecraft carried nearly 6,500 pounds of cargo.

title: Curiosity Shakes Loose a Pesky Rock
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover successfully removed a stuck rock from its drill after days of troubleshooting. The rock detached on May 1, 2026, following repositioning and vibration attempts.

title: Hubble Sights Galaxy in Transition
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy with a bright center and hints of a spiral structure, though it lacks distinct spiral arms. The image shows reddish-brown dust filaments obscuring parts of the galaxy, with background light from distant galaxies visible.

title: Picturing Earth in a New Light
summary: A recent analysis mapped changes in artificial light at night on Earth, highlighting areas where nighttime illumination has increased or decreased. The study provides new insights into global light pollution trends.

title: NASA Draws on Industry for Mars Telecommunications Network
summary: NASA has issued a Request for Proposal (RFP) to collaborate with industry on the Mars Telecommunications Network, which aims to provide reliable, high-bandwidth communications for future Mars missions. The network will utilize high-performance orbiters to support surface, orbital, and human exploration.

title: NASA, Industry Prepare Cryogenic Fuel Technology Demo
summary: NASA is partnering with Eta Space to test cryogenic fuel technology in orbit through the LOXSAT mission, aiming to enable future in-space propellant depots for deep space missions.

title: Fresh Food Delivery for Space Station
summary: NASA astronauts and ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot enjoyed fresh food delivered by Northrop Grumman's Cygnus XL cargo spacecraft to the International Space Station. The shipment included oranges, apples, onions, and other fresh produce.

title: Ice Moves Out of Aniak
summary: Ice jams and flooding occurred along Alaska’s Kuskokwim River due to spring melt. The event highlights the impact of seasonal changes on river systems in the region.

title: NASA-Supported Space Tech Advances Earthly Construction
summary: NASA-supported 3D printing technology developed for lunar habitats is being adapted by Branch Technology Inc. to create efficient and strong buildings on Earth using lightweight lattice structures.

title: Rise Goes to Washington
summary: The Artemis II zero gravity indicator 'Rise' was displayed during a congressional meeting in Washington with NASA and CSA astronauts. The mission involved a nearly 10-day journey with astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and Jeremy Hansen.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage, which has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company surpassed all its 2025 milestones.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the International Space Station (ISS) on its third attempt. The mission follows the docking of Progress MS-34 to the station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and Chinese missions are scheduled for nine launches during the week of May 11. The launches span multiple locations in the United States and China.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 completes WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 has completed a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19 successfully performed a full-duration, full-thrust static fire test with 33 engines on May 7, 2026.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing deep space missions. The country's commercial sector is also making strides toward reusability in spaceflight.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 at a launch site is undergoing conversion, with Flight 12 approaching. The upgrade follows the conclusion of Flight 11 six months prior.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission, potentially marking a calm period before an upcoming surge in spaceflight activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched its third rideshare mission of 2026 aboard a Falcon 9 rocket, carrying the CAS500-2 satellite and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency test and validate materials for spacecraft to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds to support ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance both technical and educational aspects of space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test or bake-out procedure is mentioned. The specific details of the event are not provided in the given text.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for a Mars mission, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to share feedback on space sector topics during its annual SME Forum 2026 in Paris. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April-May and in-person sessions on June 8-9, 2026.

title: Intuitive Machines Bets on Lunar Comms with Goonhilly Buy
summary: Intuitive Machines acquired UK-based Goonhilly Earth Station and its US subsidiary COMSAT to bolster its lunar communications and deep-space services. The move aims to provide integrated and resilient solutions for customers' communication needs.

title: Iridium Boosts Air Safety Offering With Aireon Acquisition
summary: Iridium has acquired Aireon to enhance its air safety services by integrating space-based VHF communications. This acquisition aims to strengthen Iridium's offerings in aviation safety and tracking.

title: European Space Startups Jockey for ‘Infrastructure’ Financing
summary: European space startups are seeking infrastructure financing to advance their growth-stage projects. The region faces challenges in securing sufficient capital for scaling up space ventures.

title: Guetlein: CBO is ‘Not Estimating What We’re Building’
summary: The head of the Golden Dome program criticized the Congressional Budget Office's $1.2 trillion cost estimate for the missile-defense program, stating it does not accurately reflect what they are building. The program is part of missile defense efforts.

title: Meet Blacknight Space Labs In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator
summary: Blacknight Space Labs is launching an In-Orbit Infrastructure Accelerator offering up to $100,000 to companies, along with mentorship and support for defense market entry and fundraising. The program also provides guidance on government contracting and customer acquisition.

title: NASA Lays Out Preliminary Artemis III Mission Plan
summary: NASA has outlined a preliminary plan for the Artemis III mission, scheduled for 2027, which will test key capabilities in low Earth orbit before a planned Moon landing in 2028.

title: Exclusive: LEAP Launches its First Rocket
summary: LEAP has successfully launched its first rocket, marking a significant milestone for the company. This event introduces a new player in the space launch sector.

title: CBO Puts $1.2T Price Tag on Golden Dome
summary: The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) estimates the cost of the Golden Dome program at $1.2 trillion, significantly higher than the $185 billion figure proposed by Gen. Michael Guetlein in March.

title: Exobiosphere, Voyager Partner on ISS Mission
summary: Exobiosphere has partnered with Voyager to deploy an Orbital High-Throughput Screening Device on the ISS. The automated mini lab will fit into standard mid-deck lockers for use on crewed or uncrewed space stations.

title: NASA’s Launch Director Pick Turns Political
summary: Brian Hughes, NASA's first senior launch operations director, previously served as the agency's chief of staff before departing. His appointment follows the transition in NASA's leadership under Administrator Jared Isaacman.

title: What Will the Golden Dome Cost? Gen. Guetlein Pushes Back on CBO Cost Estimate
summary: Gen. Guetlein, head of the Golden Dome program, disputed a Congressional Budget Office (CBO) cost estimate for the program. The disagreement highlights concerns over the financial assessment of the system proposed under President Trump's administration.

title: IFC Market in China and India is On Cusp of Growth, Valour Predicts
summary: Valour Consultancy predicts significant growth for the In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) market in China and India. The latest report highlights the potential scale and expansion of these markets.

title: Cowboy Space Files for 20,000-Satellite Constellation for Data Center Services
summary: Cowboy Space has filed for a 20,000-satellite constellation to offer data center services from space, following a $275 million Series B funding round. The company, previously rebranded, aims to leverage this massive satellite network for space-based data processing.

title: Iceye Delivers SAR Capability to Polish Armed Forces
summary: Iceye has delivered control over a radar satellite constellation to the Polish Armed Forces for sovereign intelligence within 12 months. This marks a significant step in enhancing Poland's space-based surveillance capabilities.

title: Kymeta Terminals to be Integrated Into Red Cat Uncrewed Surface Vessels
summary: Kymeta is partnering with Red Cat Futures to integrate its satcom terminals into uncrewed surface vessels. The collaboration was announced on May 14.

title: Major US Carries Band Together in Joint Venture to Encourage D2D Competition
summary: The three major US mobile carriers—AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon—have formed a joint venture to collaborate on direct-to-device (D2D) services, aiming to enhance competition in this emerging market.

title: Intuitive Machines Boosts Deep Space Communications With Goonhilly Acquisition
summary: Intuitive Machines has acquired Goonhilly Earth Station, a U.K.-based provider of deep space communication services. This acquisition aims to enhance Intuitive Machines' capabilities in deep space communications.

title: Iridium to Fully Acquire Aireon, Points to Space-Based VHF Opportunity
summary: Iridium is fully acquiring its partner Aireon, which operates a space-based air traffic surveillance system using payloads on Iridium’s network. The acquisition highlights a growing opportunity in space-based VHF (Very High Frequency) technology for air traffic management.

title: Space42 Reports Space Services Revenue Growth Despite Flat Results
summary: Space42 reported flat overall revenues but significant growth in space services revenue for the quarter ending March. The company's financial performance showed a contrast between stable total earnings and rising sector-specific income.

title: SpinLaunch to Collaborate with Aalyria for Network Orchestration
summary: SpinLaunch is collaborating with Aalyria Technologies to develop infrastructure for SpinLaunch's low-cost Meridian LEO communications constellation. Aalyria will act as a strategic partner in this effort.
